title: German Curry Chicken
description: A twist on traditional German cuisine, this dish combines the flavors of India with classic German comfort. Chicken cooked in a rich curry sauce, served with a side of bitter greens.
instructions: Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Add curry paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add beer to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the chicken to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken cooks, boil the potatoes until tender. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, sauté the bitter greens with olive oil until wilted.
To serve, slice the chicken and serve with potatoes, bitter greens, and garnish with parsley.
Serve with a side of Romantic red onion salad, if desired.
